steps.title: Notice the same line
steps.content:
The second equation is the first equation multiplied by $4$.
$$
4(7x+6y)=4(5)
$$
$$
28x+24y=20
$$
So any point on $7x+6y=5$ lies on both graphs.

steps.title: Use the parameter as y
steps.content:
Let $y=r$ in the simpler equation and solve for $x$.
$$
7x+6r=5
$$
$$
7x=5-6r
$$
$$
x=-\frac{6r}{7}+\frac{5}{7}
$$

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Define $L(x,y)=7x+6y$ and test each parameterized point. The matching point form keeps $L(x,y)=5$ for every value of $r$. The answer is D.
